4.1.title: The paramparā chain of three transmissions
4.1.body: Three finite verbs encode the lineage: प्रोक्तवान् (कृदन्त + अहम् = 'I taught', past), प्राह (परोक्ष लिट् of √ब्रू, 'told'), and अब्रवीत् (लङ् of √ब्रू, 'said'). The dative विवस्वते, मनवे, इक्ष्वाकवे form a chain of recipients, while अव्ययम् (द्वितीया, agreeing with इमं योगम्) insists this yoga is imperishable across that chain.

4.1.title: विवस्वते — sun-god as first listener
4.1.body: विवस्वते is चतुर्थी एकवचन ('to Vivasvān'), making Sūrya the original श्रोता. The compound परम्पराप्राप्तम् (which arrives in 4.2) is foreshadowed here: Kṛṣṇa frames himself not as inventor but as primal teacher, and the verb प्रोक्तवान् with क्तवतु suffix marks completed personal agency — 'it was I who spoke this'.

4.2.title: परम्पराप्राप्तम् — the lineage compound dissected
4.2.body: परम्पराप्राप्तम् is a तृतीया-तत्पुरुष ('obtained by succession'), द्वितीया एकवचन agreeing with इमं योगम्. The verb विदुः (परोक्ष लिट् बहुवचन of √विद्, 'they knew') takes राजर्षयः as प्रथमा subject; then नष्टः (कृदन्त, प्रथमा एकवचन) declares the yoga 'lost' — कालेन (तृतीया, 'by time') and महता (qualifier) supply the agent of its disappearance.

4.2.title: परन्तप — vocative of the warrior-king address
4.2.body: परन्तप is सम्बोधन, a बहुव्रीहि literally 'one whose [heat] scorches enemies'. Note the syntactic break: स (पुं. प्रथमा, referring to योग) ... नष्टः agrees in liṅga and vacana, but the relative-correlative structure is loose — कालेन इह महता ('here, by great time') localises the loss in the human realm even as the truth itself is timeless.

4.3.title: स एव अयं — that very same yoga
4.3.body: The double demonstrative स एव अयम् ('that very this one') with मया (तृतीया, 'by me') and ते (चतुर्थी/षष्ठी, 'to/for you') frames the present teaching as identical with the primordial one. प्रोक्तः is कृदन्त प्रथमा, agreeing with योगः, and पुरातनः ('ancient') reinforces that what Arjuna hears is not novel doctrine but recovered antiquity.

4.3.title: भक्तः असि मे सखा च — the credential clause
4.3.body: Two reasons given for the disclosure: भक्तः (प्रथमा) and सखा (प्रथमा of सखि, irregular) — devotee and friend. The finite असि (लट्, मध्यम पुरुष एकवचन of √अस्) anchors both predicates. रहस्यं ह्येतदुत्तमम् makes the teaching itself the object of a hidden कथ्यते: 'because this is the highest secret' — only the qualified listener unlocks the highest content.

4.4.title: अपरम् / परम् — Arjuna's chronological puzzle
4.4.body: Arjuna pairs अपरम् ('later') with भवतः (षष्ठी, 'your') and परम् ('earlier/prior') with विवस्वतः (षष्ठी). The optative विजानीयाम् (विधिलिङ्, उत्तम एकवचन of √ज्ञा) carries the perplexity: 'how should I understand this?' The quoted इति clause with प्रोक्तवान् (कृदन्त, masc. नो.) closes the report — Arjuna treats Kṛṣṇa's claim as a proposition to be examined.

4.4.title: आदौ — the locative of beginning
4.4.body: आदौ is सप्तमी ('at the beginning'), the temporal anchor of the doubt. Note how Arjuna does not deny — he simply asks 'how can it be known'. This is the classical pedagogical hinge: a student's question is what licenses the teacher's avatāra doctrine that follows.

4.5.title: बहूनि जन्मानि — the plural of incarnations
4.5.body: बहूनि (प्रथमा बहुवचन नपुंसक) qualifies जन्मानि (प्रथमा बहुवचन), and व्यतीतानि is कृदन्त agreeing fully. मे and तव are षष्ठी ('of me, of you'). The contrast hinges on two verbs of knowing: वेद (परोक्ष लिट् of √विद्, उत्तम एकवचन, 'I know') vs. वेत्थ (परोक्ष लिट् मध्यम एकवचन, 'you do not know') — same root, asymmetric memory.

4.5.title: तानि सर्वाणि — the totalising pronoun
4.5.body: तानि सर्वाणि (द्वितीया बहुवचन नपुंसक) is the object of वेद, asserting omniscient recall across all births. The vocative परन्तप mirrors 4.2 but with reversed pragmatic force: Arjuna the 'enemy-scorcher' is told he cannot scorch the veil over his own past lives — only Kṛṣṇa can.

4.6.title: अजः अपि सन् — the participle-concession
4.6.body: सन् is शतृ कृदन्त of √अस् ('being'), and अजः अपि / ईश्वरः अपि form a double concessive: 'though unborn, though Lord'. The finite verb सम्भवामि (लट्, उत्तम एकवचन of सम्+√भू, 'I come into being') is the paradox itself, modulated by आत्ममाययाय (तृतीया, 'by my own māyā').

4.6.title: प्रकृतिं स्वाम् अधिष्ठाय — the absolutive of mastery
4.6.body: अधिष्ठाय is ल्यप् absolutive of अधि+√स्था ('having mounted/presided over'), and प्रकृतिम् स्वाम् is द्वितीया एकवचन ('over my own prakṛti'). The avatāra is not entrapment in matter but voluntary command of it — the absolutive grammatically subordinates prakṛti to the divine subject.

4.7.title: यदा यदा हि — the iterated correlative
4.7.body: The doubled यदा यदा ('whenever, whenever') with हि ('indeed') sets up a habitual correlative completed by तदा. ग्लानिः (प्रथमा एकवचन स्त्री, 'decline') and अभ्युत्थानम् (प्रथमा एकवचन नपुंसक, 'uprising') are paired subjects; धर्मस्य and अधर्मस्य are षष्ठी, giving the precise grammatical contrast: decline-of-dharma and rise-of-adharma.

4.7.title: आत्मानं सृजामि — reflexive self-creation
4.7.body: सृजामि is लट् उत्तम एकवचन of √सृज् ('I emit/create'), and आत्मानम् is द्वितीया एकवचन reflexive — 'I emit my own self'. The vocative भारत anchors the cosmic claim in personal address; the act of avatāra is not generation by another but a self-issuing of the same divine subject.

4.8.title: Three datives of purpose
4.8.body: परित्राणाय, विनाशाय, धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय are all चतुर्थी एकवचन of purpose, paralleling साधूनां and दुष्कृताम् (षष्ठी बहुवचन). The triple चतुर्थी is grammatically the engine of the verse: protection, destruction, re-establishment — three goals stacked, governing the single finite सम्भवामि.

4.8.title: युगे युगे — the locative of recurrence
4.8.body: युगे युगे (सप्तमी repeated) gives 'in age after age', mirroring 4.7's doubled यदा. सम्भवामि (लट् of सम्+√भू) recurs from 4.6 but now in iterative aspect — the avatāra is not a one-off but a structural feature of cosmic time.

4.9.title: जन्म कर्म च दिव्यम् — the predicate adjective
4.9.body: जन्म and कर्म are प्रथमा/द्वितीया एकवचन (here द्वितीया, governed by वेत्ति), and दिव्यम् qualifies both as 'divine'. The relative यः ... सः and the participle त्यक्त्वा (ktvā absolutive, 'having abandoned [the body]') frame a future: न एति (लट् with negation, 'does not go again') vs. माम् एति ('comes to me').

4.9.title: तत्त्वतः — the ablative-suffixed adverb
4.9.body: तत्त्वतः ends in -तस् (an ablative-sense suffix forming adverbs, 'in truth, essentially'). The whole construction यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः specifies the epistemic quality: not 'who knows that' but 'who knows truly'. Kṛṣṇa's promise is conditioned on the *manner* of knowing, not the bare fact.

4.10.title: वीतरागभयक्रोधाः — the chain बहुव्रीहि
4.10.body: वीतरागभयक्रोधाः is a बहुव्रीहि (प्रथमा बहुवचन पुं.) — 'those whose desire, fear, and anger have departed', with वीत (कृदन्त of √इ, 'gone away') prefixed to the dvandva. मन्मयाः ('made of me') and उपाश्रिताः (कृदन्त) extend the predicate stack.

4.10.title: मद्भावम् आगताः — the destination compound
4.10.body: मद्भावम् is द्वितीया एकवचन (object of आगताः, कृदन्त of आ+√गम् 'come to'), literally 'come to my-state'. ज्ञानतपसा (तृतीया एकवचन, 'by the austerity of knowledge') is the means, and पूताः (कृदन्त, 'purified') makes the purification logically prior to the arrival.

4.11.title: ये यथा ... तांस्तथा — proportional correlative
4.11.body: The relative pair यथा/तथा with ये/तान् sets up exact proportionality: 'in whatever manner they approach, in that same manner I requite'. प्रपद्यन्ते is लट् आत्मनेपद बहुवचन of प्र+√पद् ('they take refuge'); भजामि is लट् उत्तम एकवचन ('I share/serve').

4.11.title: मम वर्त्म अनुवर्तन्ते — following the path
4.11.body: वर्त्म is द्वितीया एकवचन नपुंसक ('path'), and अनुवर्तन्ते (लट् of अनु+√वृत्) literally 'they turn-after / follow'. सर्वशः (adverbial -शस् suffix, 'in every way') universalises: the claim is not sectarian — all paths to Kṛṣṇa proceed by Kṛṣṇa's own structure.

4.12.title: काङ्क्षन्तः — the active शतृ participle
4.12.body: काङ्क्षन्तः is शतृ कृदन्त प्रथमा बहुवचन of √काङ्क्ष् ('desiring'), governing सिद्धिम् (द्वितीया, 'success') with कर्मणाम् (षष्ठी, 'of works'). यजन्ते (लट् आत्मनेपद, 'they worship/sacrifice to') takes देवताः as object — a deliberate hint that ritual fruits operate on a lower, quicker timescale.

4.12.title: क्षिप्रं ... कर्मजा — adverb plus adjective
4.12.body: क्षिप्रम् is adverbial ('quickly') and कर्मजा is प्रथमा एकवचन स्त्री (qualifying सिद्धिः) — 'born of action'. मानुषे लोके is सप्तमी ('in the human world'), localising the verse's diagnosis: results-driven karma works fast in the human plane, but its very speed marks its limit.

4.13.title: गुणकर्मविभागशः — the adverbial -शस्
4.13.body: गुणकर्मविभागशः is a samāhāra-tatpuruṣa ending in -शस् (adverbial, 'by the division of'), spelling out the principle of चातुर्वर्ण्यम् (नपुंसक, 'four-class system'). सृष्टम् is कृदन्त नपुंसक prathamā, agreeing with the abstract noun and constructed with मया (तृतीया, 'by me').

4.13.title: कर्तारम् अपि / अकर्तारम् अव्ययम् — the paradox compound
4.13.body: कर्तारम् and अकर्तारम् are both द्वितीया एकवचन of agent-nouns in -तृ (टा), held in tension by अपि. The imperative विद्धि (लोट् मध्यम एकवचन of √विद्, 'know!') addresses Arjuna directly: know me as both the maker and the not-maker — the अव्ययम् ('imperishable') overrides the apparent contradiction.

4.14.title: लिम्पन्ति / स्पृहा — verb plus noun parallel
4.14.body: लिम्पन्ति is लट् बहुवचन of √लिप् ('they smear, stain') with कर्माणि (प्रथमा बहुवचन नपुंसक) as subject and माम् (द्वितीया) as object. The second hemistich shifts to a nominal sentence: स्पृहा (प्रथमा एकवचन स्त्री, 'craving') with implied अस्ति and कर्मफले (सप्तमी, 'in the fruit of action').

4.14.title: इति — the quotative knower
4.14.body: इति closes the propositional content of what is to be 'directly known' (अभिजानाति, लट् of अभि+√ज्ञा). The result-clause uses the passive बध्यते (लट् कर्मणि, 'is bound'), with कर्मभिः (तृतीया बहुवचन, 'by works') as instrumental — knowing this proposition unbinds, ignorance binds.

4.15.title: एवं ज्ञात्वा कृतं कर्म — absolutive plus participle
4.15.body: ज्ञात्वा is ktvā absolutive of √ज्ञा, and कृतम् is कृदन्त (नपुंसक एकवचन, agreeing with कर्म) — 'action having been done by ancients having known thus'. पूर्वैः मुमुक्षुभिः is तृतीया बहुवचन ('by former seekers of liberation', from √मुच्).

4.15.title: कुरु कर्मैव — the imperative of urgency
4.15.body: कुरु is लोट् मध्यम एकवचन of √कृ ('do!'), and कर्म एव is द्वितीया with restrictive एव ('precisely action'). पूर्वतरम् (comparative -तर of पूर्व, 'earlier-still') intensifies the appeal to antiquity: do as the very earliest did — the imperative is grounded in lineage, not novelty.

4.16.title: किं कर्म किम् अकर्म — the embedded question
4.16.body: Two interrogative किम् clauses with कर्म (action) and अकर्म (non-action, formed with privative न), closed by इति, become the object of मोहिताः (कृदन्त बहुवचन प्रथमा, 'are bewildered'). Even कवयः ('the wise', प्रथमा बहुवचन) — the very people one would consult — are deluded here.

4.16.title: यत् ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसे — the future of release
4.16.body: यत् is द्वितीया, antecedent to कर्म in the previous line; ज्ञात्वा is absolutive; मोक्ष्यसे is लृट् मध्यम एकवचन of √मुच् कर्मणि ('you shall be released'), with अशुभात् (पञ्चमी एकवचन, 'from evil/the inauspicious'). The -ष्य- infix is the standard लृट् signal.

4.17.title: Three datives of obligation — बोद्धव्यम्
4.17.body: बोद्धव्यम् is तव्यत् कृदन्त ('to be understood'), repeated three times in a triplet: of कर्मणः (षष्ठी, 'of action'), of विकर्मणः ('of wrong-action'), and of अकर्मणः ('of inaction'). The गहना (प्रथमा एकवचन स्त्री, 'deep, impenetrable') predicates कर्मणः गतिः: the *course* of action is hard to fathom.

4.17.title: गहना कर्मणो गतिः — the diagnostic line
4.17.body: गतिः (प्रथमा एकवचन स्त्री, from √गम्) means 'movement, course, ultimate destiny'. This whole verse functions as the methodological warning before 4.18's central paradox: don't approach the कर्म/अकर्म distinction superficially.

4.18.title: कर्मणि अकर्म ... अकर्मणि कर्म — chiastic locatives
4.18.body: Both कर्मणि and अकर्मणि are सप्तमी एकवचन ('in action, in inaction'), with their counterparts in द्वितीया (object of पश्येत्, विधिलिङ् of √दृश्, 'should see'). The chiasmus is the heart of karma-yoga: see non-action inside action, and action inside non-action.

4.18.title: स बुद्धिमान् ... कृत्स्नकर्मकृत्
4.18.body: बुद्धिमान् is -मत् possessive ('intelligent') and युक्तः is कृदन्त ('joined, yoked'). कृत्स्नकर्मकृत् is a कर्म-तत्पुरुष ('whole-action-doer', कृत् from √कृ) — paradoxically, the one who *sees* inaction within action is the one who is the *whole* doer of action.

4.19.title: कामसङ्कल्पवर्जिताः — privative compound
4.19.body: यस्य is षष्ठी relative, referring to the agent. कामसङ्कल्पवर्जिताः is a तृतीया-तत्पुरुष ending in वर्जित (कृदन्त, 'devoid of'): 'devoid of desire-and-volition'. सर्वे समारम्भाः (प्रथमा बहुवचन, 'all undertakings') is the subject.

4.19.title: ज्ञानाग्निदग्धकर्माणम् — the fire compound
4.19.body: ज्ञानाग्निदग्धकर्माणम् is a multi-step compound: ज्ञान-अग्नि = 'fire of knowledge' (षष्ठी-तत्पुरुष), then दग्ध (कृदन्त) qualifies कर्म ('actions burnt by the fire of knowledge'), and the whole becomes a बहुव्रीहि in द्वितीया एकवचन ('him whose karmas are burnt...'). The verb आहुः (परोक्ष लिट् बहुवचन of √अह्, 'they call') governs तम् ... पण्डितम्.

4.20.title: त्यक्त्वा ... नित्यतृप्तः निराश्रयः — absolutive plus predicates
4.20.body: त्यक्त्वा (ktvā of √त्यज्) governs कर्मफलासङ्गम् (द्वितीया, 'attachment to fruit'). नित्यतृप्तः ('ever-content') and निराश्रयः ('without support', बहुव्रीहि with निर्-) stack as predicates of the agent.

4.20.title: अभिप्रवृत्तः अपि — concessive participle
4.20.body: अभिप्रवृत्तः is कृदन्त of अभि+प्र+√वृत् ('thoroughly engaged'), and कर्मणि is सप्तमी ('in action'). The concessive अपि sets up the paradox: न एव किञ्चित् करोति सः ('he does not really do anything'). करोति is लट् of √कृ; the verse uses ordinary grammar to make a metaphysical point.

4.21.title: निराशीः यतचित्तात्मा त्यक्तसर्वपरिग्रहः — three predicate compounds
4.21.body: निराशीः ('without expectation', बहुव्रीहि), यतचित्तात्मा ('one whose mind-and-self are restrained', a chain compound), त्यक्तसर्वपरिग्रहः ('one who has given up all possession') — three predicates of the agent. The triple stacking is the karma-yogi's specification.

4.21.title: शारीरम् केवलं कर्म — bodily action alone
4.21.body: शारीरम् qualifies कर्म (नपुंसक एकवचन, द्वितीया) — 'merely bodily action'. कुर्वन् is शतृ कृदन्त ('doing'). न आप्नोति किल्बिषम् (लट् of आ+√आप् + द्वितीया, 'does not incur sin') — bodily action without psychic engagement does not accumulate karmic residue.

4.22.title: यदृच्छालाभसन्तुष्टः — the chance-gain compound
4.22.body: यदृच्छालाभसन्तुष्टः is a long तृतीया-तत्पुरुष: यदृच्छा-लाभ ('gain by chance') + सन्तुष्ट (कृदन्त, 'satisfied') = 'satisfied by whatever-comes gain'. द्वन्द्वातीतः ('transcending the pairs') and विमत्सरः ('without envy') are further predicates.

4.22.title: समः सिद्धौ असिद्धौ च — equal in both locatives
4.22.body: सिद्धौ and असिद्धौ are सप्तमी एकवचन (sing. fem. -i stem, 'in success, in failure'). कृत्वा अपि (ktvā plus concessive अपि) — 'even having acted' — meets न निबध्यते (लट् कर्मणि of नि+√बन्ध्, 'is not bound'). The same passive of binding appears in 4.14 and 4.23 — a thematic refrain.

4.23.title: गतसङ्गस्य मुक्तस्य — the genitive chain
4.23.body: गतसङ्गस्य (षष्ठी एकवचन, बहुव्रीहि 'one whose attachment has gone'), मुक्तस्य (कृदन्त genitive, 'of the released'), and ज्ञानावस्थितचेतसः (षष्ठी बहुव्रीहि, 'one whose mind is established in knowledge') — three genitives all qualifying the same agent.

4.23.title: यज्ञाय आचरतः — dative of purpose with participle
4.23.body: यज्ञाय is चतुर्थी एकवचन ('for sacrifice'), and आचरतः is शतृ कृदन्त षष्ठी ('of one acting'). The verb प्रविलीयते (लट् कर्मणि of प्र+वि+√ली, 'is wholly dissolved') with समग्रम् ('entirely') governing कर्म — action done for yajña dissolves into its own sacrificial frame.

4.24.title: Five-fold ब्रह्म — the most compact yajña formula
4.24.body: ब्रह्मार्पणम् (the offering-vessel), ब्रह्म हविः (the oblation), ब्रह्म-अग्नौ (सप्तमी, 'in the fire that is brahman'), ब्रह्मणा (तृतीया, 'by brahman'), हुतम् (कृदन्त, 'offered'). Five grammatical cases of the same noun ब्रह्मन्, dramatising the non-duality of every sacrificial element.

4.24.title: ब्रह्मकर्मसमाधिना — the means-ending instrumental
4.24.body: ब्रह्मकर्मसमाधिना is तृतीया एकवचन of a chain compound — 'by the absorption in brahman-as-action'. गन्तव्यम् is तव्यत् कृदन्त ('to be reached'), with तेन (तृतीया, 'by him/that') as agent and ब्रह्म एव (प्रथमा with restrictive एव) as the destination — same noun, all five grammatical positions.

4.25.title: दैवम् यज्ञम् — the deity-directed sacrifice
4.25.body: दैवम् ('to the gods', adjectival द्वितीया) qualifies यज्ञम्, and पर्युपासते (लट् आत्मनेपद बहुवचन of परि+उप+√आस्, 'they attend on/worship around') is the finite verb. अपरे ('others') is one of a series of अपरे...अपरे distributives that organise verses 4.25-30.

4.25.title: ब्रह्माग्नौ ... यज्ञेन एव उपजुह्वति
4.25.body: ब्रह्माग्नौ is सप्तमी ('in the fire of brahman'), and यज्ञेन एव is तृतीया with restrictive एव ('by sacrifice alone'). उपजुह्वति is लट् बहुवचन of उप+√हु ('they offer near') — the higher class of yogins offer sacrifice *as* the means *into* the brahmic fire.

4.26.title: श्रोत्रादीनि इन्द्रियाणि — the आदि compound
4.26.body: श्रोत्रादीनि is a बहुव्रीहि नपुंसक बहुवचन ('hearing-etc.'), qualifying इन्द्रियाणि (द्वितीया बहुवचन). The first hemistich sacrifices the *senses* into संयमाग्निषु (सप्तमी बहुवचन, 'in the fires of restraint') — note the plural: each sense has its own fire of restraint.

4.26.title: शब्दादीन् विषयान् — sense-objects into sense-fires
4.26.body: Mirror image: शब्दादीन् (द्वितीया, 'sound-etc.') qualifies विषयान् ('objects'); इन्द्रियाग्निषु (सप्तमी बहुवचन, 'in sense-fires'). जुह्वति (लट् बहुवचन of √हु) repeats both lines. The two hemistichs map the two yogic moves: withdraw senses, or consume objects through them.

4.27.title: सर्वाणि इन्द्रियकर्माणि — totalising compound
4.27.body: सर्वाणि (नपुंसक बहुवचन) qualifies इन्द्रियकर्माणि (षष्ठी-तत्पुरुष, 'actions of the senses') and प्राणकर्माणि ('actions of the breaths'), both द्वितीया बहुवचन. अपरे continues the distributive listing.

4.27.title: आत्मसंयमयोगाग्नौ ज्ञानदीपिते
4.27.body: आत्मसंयमयोगाग्नौ is सप्तमी एकवचन of a long compound ('in the fire of the yoga of self-restraint'), and ज्ञानदीपिते is कृदन्त सप्तमी ('lit/kindled by knowledge') — the qualifying participle agrees with अग्नौ. The yogāgni is fed by *all* operations of body and breath, kindled by jñāna.

4.28.title: Four kinds of yajña — the dvandva listing
4.28.body: द्रव्ययज्ञाः ('sacrifice of substance'), तपोयज्ञाः ('of austerity'), योगयज्ञाः ('of yoga'), स्वाध्यायज्ञानयज्ञाः ('of self-study and knowledge') — four प्रथमा बहुवचन compounds, each a कर्मधारय. They are united as predicates of यतयः ('strivers').

4.28.title: संशितव्रताः — the sharpened-vows बहुव्रीहि
4.28.body: संशितव्रताः is बहुव्रीहि प्रथमा बहुवचन ('those whose vows are sharpened/intensified'), with संशित कृदन्त of सम्+√शो. The verse refuses to rank these yajñas — its grammar lists them in parallel, leaving the qualifier 'sharpened vows' as the common spec.

4.29.title: अपाने ... प्राणे — locatives of breath
4.29.body: अपाने and प्राणे are सप्तमी एकवचन; प्राणम् and अपानम् are द्वितीया. जुह्वति (लट् of √हु) is repeated, with prāṇa offered into apāna and apāna into prāṇa — two reciprocal directions of breath-sacrifice.

4.29.title: प्राणापानगती रुद्ध्वा — absolutive of cessation
4.29.body: रुद्ध्वा is ktvā of √रुध् ('having checked/blocked'), governing प्राणापानगती (द्वितीया द्विवचन स्त्री, 'the two courses of prāṇa-apāna'). प्राणायामपरायणाः is बहुव्रीहि ('those wholly devoted to prāṇāyāma') — the verse describes kumbhaka-style breath-yajña.

4.30.title: नियताहाराः — the controlled-food बहुव्रीहि
4.30.body: नियताहाराः is बहुव्रीहि बहुवचन ('those whose food is regulated'). प्राणान् प्राणेषु जुह्वति: द्वितीया बहुवचन into सप्तमी बहुवचन — the breaths into the breaths. The variation across 4.26-30 quietly catalogues a complete typology of prāṇic and ritual disciplines.

4.30.title: यज्ञक्षपितकल्मषाः — the sin-emaciated compound
4.30.body: यज्ञक्षपितकल्मषाः is a complex बहुव्रीहि: यज्ञ-क्षपित ('worn down by sacrifice', कृदन्त of क्षि) + कल्मष ('sin') = 'those whose sins are worn down by sacrifice'. सर्वे एते यज्ञविदः sums up: all of these are knowers of yajña, irrespective of which specific form.

4.31.title: यज्ञशिष्टामृतभुजः — the eater-of-leftovers
4.31.body: यज्ञशिष्टामृतभुजः is a long compound: यज्ञ-शिष्ट ('left over from sacrifice') + अमृत ('nectar') + भुज् ('eaters'), all in प्रथमा बहुवचन. They यान्ति (लट् of √या, 'they go') to ब्रह्म सनातनम् (द्वितीया एकवचन, 'eternal brahman').

4.31.title: न अयं लोकः ... कुतः अन्यः — the rhetorical escalation
4.31.body: अयजस्य is षष्ठी एकवचन of an अयज्ञ-stem ('of the non-sacrificer'), but here read अयज्ञस्य. The construction न अस्ति ... कुतः अन्यः ('this world is not... how then another?') uses the कुतः-rhetorical to escalate: if not this world, certainly not the higher one. The vocative कुरुसत्तम pins the address to Arjuna.

4.32.title: बहुविधाः यज्ञाः — the diversity adjective
4.32.body: बहुविधाः (प्रथमा बहुवचन, 'many-kinded') qualifies यज्ञाः, and वितताः (कृदन्त, 'spread out') is the predicate participle. ब्रह्मणः मुखे is षष्ठी + सप्तमी ('in the mouth of brahman') — yajñas as spread before the very face of brahman.

4.32.title: कर्मजान् ... विमोक्ष्यसे — imperative plus future
4.32.body: विद्धि (लोट् मध्यम एकवचन of √विद्, 'know!') governs कर्मजान् (द्वितीया बहुवचन, 'born of action') and तान् सर्वान्. The result clause uses विमोक्ष्यसे (लृट् मध्यम एकवचन of वि+√मुच् कर्मणि, 'you shall be wholly released') — the -ष्य- future signals the soteriological payoff.

4.33.title: श्रेयान् द्रव्यमयात् — the comparative ablative
4.33.body: श्रेयान् is प्रथमा एकवचन of the comparative श्रेयस् ('better'), governing the पञ्चमी द्रव्यमयात् यज्ञात् ('than the substance-made sacrifice'). ज्ञानयज्ञः (प्रथमा) is the elevated subject.

4.33.title: ज्ञाने परिसमाप्यते — the locative of completion
4.33.body: सर्वम् ... कर्म अखिलम् ('all action, in its entirety') is the subject of परिसमाप्यते (लट् कर्मणि of परि+सम्+√आप्, 'is wholly completed'). ज्ञाने is सप्तमी ('in knowledge') — knowledge is where every karma comes to rest. The passive voice deliberately defocuses the agent.

4.34.title: प्रणिपातेन परिप्रश्नेन सेवया — three तृतीयाs
4.34.body: Three तृतीया एकवचन instrumentals: प्रणिपातेन ('by prostration'), परिप्रश्नेन ('by thorough questioning'), सेवया ('by service'). All three modify तत् विद्धि (लोट् of √विद्), specifying the *manner* of approaching ज्ञान.

4.34.title: उपदेक्ष्यन्ति — the future of teaching
4.34.body: उपदेक्ष्यन्ति is लृट् बहुवचन of उप+√दिश् ('they will teach'), with ते (चतुर्थी, 'to you') and ज्ञानिनः तत्त्वदर्शिनः (प्रथमा बहुवचन, 'the knowers, the seers-of-truth'). The plural is significant: not one guru but the class of qualified teachers.

4.35.title: यत् ज्ञात्वा न पुनः मोहम् यास्यसि
4.35.body: यत् is द्वितीया (object of ज्ञात्वा absolutive), and the result clause uses यास्यसि (लृट् मध्यम एकवचन of √या with मोहम्, 'you shall not go to delusion again'). The -ष्य- future and adverb पुनः together promise irreversibility.

4.35.title: द्रक्ष्यसि आत्मनि अथो मयि — locatives of vision
4.35.body: द्रक्ष्यसि is लृट् मध्यम एकवचन of √दृश् ('you shall see'). भूतानि (द्वितीया बहुवचन, 'beings') is the object, अशेषेण (तृतीया, 'without remainder') the modifier, आत्मनि / मयि सप्तमी ('in self / in me') the locations of vision — beings seen *inside* the self, then in Kṛṣṇa.

4.36.title: अपि चेद् असि ... पापकृत्तमः — the conditional concession
4.36.body: अपि चेद् ('even if') with असि (लट् of √अस्) and the superlative पापकृत्तमः (प्रथमा एकवचन, 'the very worst doer of sin' — कृत् from √कृ plus तम superlative) sets an extreme hypothesis. पापेभ्यः सर्वेभ्यः is पञ्चमी बहुवचन ('among all sinners').

4.36.title: ज्ञानप्लवेन एव — the boat instrumental
4.36.body: ज्ञानप्लवेन एव is तृतीया with restrictive एव ('by the very boat of knowledge'). सन्तरिष्यसि (लृट् मध्यम एकवचन of सम्+√तृ, 'you shall cross over') governs वृजिनम् (द्वितीया एकवचन, 'evil, crookedness'). The image — sin as river, jñāna as boat — is grammatically just तृतीया + द्वितीया.

4.37.title: यथा...तथा — fire simile in correlative
4.37.body: एधांसि (द्वितीया बहुवचन नपुंसक, 'fuel-sticks') is reduced by समिद्धः अग्निः (प्रथमा एकवचन, 'a kindled fire'); भस्मसात् कुरुते (compound verb with च्वि-style affix भस्मसात्, 'reduces to ashes', with √कृ in लट् आत्मनेपद). The vocative अर्जुन sits inside the simile.

4.37.title: ज्ञानाग्निः सर्वकर्माणि भस्मसात् कुरुते
4.37.body: Parallel construction with ज्ञानाग्निः (प्रथमा, 'fire of knowledge') and सर्वकर्माणि (द्वितीया बहुवचन). The same भस्मसात् कुरुते repeats — the simile and the proposition share grammar exactly, making the metaphor maximally legible.

4.38.title: ज्ञानेन सदृशम् पवित्रम् — comparative with तृतीया
4.38.body: ज्ञानेन is तृतीया एकवचन ('with knowledge'), and सदृशम् (नपुंसक एकवचन, 'similar to') governs the तृतीया. पवित्रम् (नपुंसक, 'a purifier') is the predicate; विद्यते (लट् कर्मणि of √विद्, 'is found') confirms nothing comparable exists 'here' (इह).

4.38.title: योगसंसिद्धः कालेन आत्मनि विन्दति
4.38.body: योगसंसिद्धः is कृदन्त ('one matured by yoga'). कालेन (तृतीया, 'in/by time') and आत्मनि (सप्तमी, 'in the self') specify when and where. विन्दति is लट् of √विद् ('he finds'), with स्वयम् ('by oneself') as the manner. The verse insists knowledge ripens internally on its own timetable.

4.39.title: श्रद्धावान् लभते — the faith-possessor verb
4.39.body: श्रद्धावान् is -मत् possessive प्रथमा एकवचन ('faithful'), and लभते is लट् आत्मनेपद of √लभ् ('he obtains'). तत्परः ('intent on that', बहुव्रीहि) and संयतेन्द्रियः ('whose senses are restrained', बहुव्रीहि) stack as further predicates.

4.39.title: ज्ञानं लब्ध्वा परां शान्तिम् अधिगच्छति
4.39.body: लब्ध्वा is ktvā of √लभ् ('having obtained'). पराम् शान्तिम् is द्वितीया स्त्री ('supreme peace'). अधिगच्छति (लट् of अधि+√गम्) is the verb of attainment. अचिरेण is तृतीया ('without delay') — the path is described as sequenced: śraddhā → jñāna → śānti, and not slow.

4.40.title: अज्ञः अश्रद्दधानः संशयात्मा — three predicates of ruin
4.40.body: अज्ञः ('ignorant'), अश्रद्दधानः (शानच् कृदन्त of श्रद् +√धा with negation, 'not having faith'), संशयात्मा ('whose self is doubt', बहुव्रीहि) — three प्रथमा subjects of विनश्यति (लट् of वि+√नश्, 'he perishes').

4.40.title: न अयं लोकः ... न परः न सुखम् — the triple negation
4.40.body: Three न-clauses with implied अस्ति: 'this world is not, nor the next, nor happiness' — all denied to संशयात्मनः (षष्ठी एकवचन, 'for the doubt-selved one'). The grammar of negation triples the loss; doubt undoes all three planes.

4.41.title: योगसंन्यस्तकर्माणम् — the renounced-action बहुव्रीहि
4.41.body: योगसंन्यस्तकर्माणम् is द्वितीया एकवचन ('him whose actions are renounced by yoga'), with संन्यस्त कृदन्त of सम्+नि+√अस्. ज्ञानसञ्छिन्नसंशयम् is parallel ('whose doubt is severed by knowledge', कृदन्त of सम्+√छिद्). आत्मवन्तम् is -मत् possessive द्वितीया ('the self-possessing one').

4.41.title: न कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति — passive-style finite verb
4.41.body: कर्माणि is प्रथमा बहुवचन subject; निबध्नन्ति is लट् बहुवचन of नि+√बन्ध् ('they do not bind'). The verb of binding recurs from 4.14 and 4.22, completing the chapter's refrain — the threefold qualification (yoga-renounced, doubt-severed, self-possessed) is what guarantees non-binding.

4.42.title: ज्ञानासिना आत्मनः — the sword instrumental
4.42.body: ज्ञानासिना is तृतीया एकवचन ('by the sword of knowledge') and आत्मनः is षष्ठी ('your own'). अज्ञानसम्भूतम् (कृदन्त, 'arisen from ignorance') and हृत्स्थम् ('seated in the heart', सप्तमी inside compound) both qualify संशयम् (द्वितीया एकवचन).

4.42.title: छित्त्वा ... योगम् आतिष्ठ उत्तिष्ठ
4.42.body: छित्त्वा is ktvā of √छिद् ('having severed'). Two imperatives close the chapter: आतिष्ठ (लोट् मध्यम एकवचन of आ+√स्था, 'stand established in [yoga]') and उत्तिष्ठ (लोट् of उत्+√स्था, 'rise up!') — the same उत्तिष्ठ that ended 2.3 and inaugurated the campaign, now returns as the chapter's closing call to action.
